briefdescription.para: Interrupt Status.
Interrupt Enable.
Transmit Reset.
Transmit Vacancy.
Transmit Data.
Axi4 Transmit Data.
Transmit Length.
Receive Reset.
Receive Occupancy.
Receive Data.
Axi4 Receive Data.
Receive Length.
Local Link Reset.
Transmit Destination.
Receive Destination.

description.para: These bits are associated with the XLLF_IER_OFFSET and XLLF_ISR_OFFSET registers.

briefdescription.para: Receive under-read.
Receive over-read.
Receive underrun (empty)
Transmit overrun.
Transmit complete.
Receive complete.
Transmit length mismatch.
Transmit reset complete.
Receive reset complete.
Tx FIFO Programmable Full, AXI FIFO MM2S Only.
Tx FIFO Programmable Empty AXI FIFO MM2S Only.
Rx FIFO Programmable Full AXI FIFO MM2S Only.
Rx FIFO Programmable Empty AXI FIFO MM2S Only.
All the ints.
Error status ints.
Receive Error status ints.
Transmit Error status ints.

description.para: These bits are associated with the XLLF_TDFR_OFFSET and XLLF_RDFR_OFFSET reset registers.

briefdescription.para: receive reset value
Transmit reset value.
Local Link reset value.
This typedef defines a run-time instance of an XLlFifo device.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* XStrm_XferFnType)(void *FifoInstance, void *BufPtr, unsigned WordCount)
memberdef.name: XStrm_XferFnType

briefdescription.para: This typedef defines a run-time instance of a receive byte-streamer.
This typedef defines a run-time instance of a transmit byte-streamer.
XLlFifo_iRxOccupancy returns the number of 32-bit words available (occupancy) to be read from the receive channel of the FIFO, specified by InstancePtr.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

simplesect.para: XLlFifo_iRxOccupancy returns the occupancy count in 32-bit words for the specified FIFO.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_iRxGetLen notifies the hardware that the program is ready to receive the next frame from the receive channel of the FIFO specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: Note that the program must first call XLlFifo_iRxGetLen before pulling data out of the receive channel of the FIFO with XLlFifo_Read.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

simplesect.para: XLlFifo_iRxGetLen returns the number of bytes available in the next frame.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_iRead_Aligned reads, WordCount, words from the FIFO referenced by InstancePtr to the block of memory, referenced by BufPtr.

detaileddescription.para: XLlFifo_iRead_Aligned assumes that BufPtr is already aligned according to the following hardware limitations: ppc - aligned on 32 bit boundaries to avoid performance penalties from unaligned exception handling. microblaze - aligned on 32 bit boundaries as microblaze does not handle unaligned transfers.

Care must be taken to ensure that the number of words read with one or more calls to XLlFifo_Read() does not exceed the number of bytes (rounded up to the nearest whole 32 bit word) available given from the last call to XLlFifo_RxGetLen().

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.
specifies the memory address to place the data read.
specifies the number of 32 bit words to read.

simplesect.para: XLlFifo_iRead_Aligned always returns XST_SUCCESS. Error handling is otherwise handled through hardware exceptions and interrupts.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_iTxVacancy returns the number of unused 32 bit words available (vacancy) in the send channel of the FIFO, specified by InstancePtr.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

simplesect.para: XLlFifo_iTxVacancy returns the vacancy count in 32-bit words for the specified FIFO.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_iTxSetLen begins a hardware transfer of data out of the transmit channel of the FIFO, specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: Bytes specifies the number of bytes in the frame to transmit.
Note that Bytes (rounded up to the nearest whole 32 bit word) must be same number of words just written using one or more calls to XLlFifo_iWrite_Aligned()

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.
specifies the number of bytes to transmit.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_iWrite_Aligned writes, WordCount, words to the FIFO referenced by InstancePtr from the block of memory, referenced by BufPtr.

detaileddescription.para: XLlFifo_iWrite_Aligned assumes that BufPtr is already aligned according to the following hardware limitations: ppc - aligned on 32 bit boundaries to avoid performance penalties from unaligned exception handling. microblaze - aligned on 32 bit boundaries as microblaze does not handle unaligned transfers.

Care must be taken to ensure that the number of words written with one or more calls to XLlFifo_iWrite_Aligned() matches the number of bytes (rounded up to the nearest whole 32 bit word) given in the next call to XLlFifo_iTxSetLen().

InstancePtr
references the FIFO on which to operate.
BufPtr
specifies the memory address to place the data read.
WordCount
specifies the number of 32 bit words to read.
XLlFifo_iWrite_Aligned always returns XST_SUCCESS. Error handling is otherwise handled through hardware exceptions and interrupts.
C Signature: int XLlFifo_iWrite_Aligned(XLlFifo *InstancePtr, void *BufPtr, unsigned WordCount);

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_CfgInitialize initializes an XPS_ll_Fifo device along with the InstancePtr that references it.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Streaming FIFO instance to be worked on.
references the structure holding the hardware configuration for the Axi Streaming FIFO core to initialize.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from EffectiveAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called. If address translation is not used, use Config->BaseAddress for this parameters, passing the physical address instead.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_RxGetWord reads one 32 bit word from the FIFO specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: XLlFifo_RxGetLen or XLlFifo_iRxGetLen must be called before calling XLlFifo_RxGetWord. Otherwise, the hardware will raise an Over Read Exception.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

simplesect.para: XLlFifo_RxGetWord returns the 32 bit word read from the FIFO.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_TxPutWord writes the 32 bit word, Word to the FIFO specified by InstancePtr.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.
is the data word to be written to FIFO.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XLlFifo_TxPutWord(XLlFifo *InstancePtr, u32 Word)

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_Initialize initializes an XPS_ll_Fifo device along with the InstancePtr that references it.

parameterdescription.para: references the memory instance to be associated with the FIFO device upon initialization.
is the processor address used to access the base address of the Fifo device.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XLlFifo_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xllfifo.h for the definition of XLlFifo_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

memberdef.argsstring: (XStrm_RxFifoStreamer *InstancePtr, unsigned FifoWidth, void *FifoInstance, XStrm_XferFnType ReadFn, XStrm_GetLenFnType GetLenFn, XStrm_GetOccupancyFnType GetOccupancyFn)
memberdef.name: XStrm_RxInitialize

briefdescription.para: XStrm_RxInitialize initializes the XStrm_RxFifoStreamer object referenced by InstancePtr.

parameterdescription.para: references the tx streamer on which to operate.
specifies the FIFO keyhole size in bytes.
references the FIFO driver instance that this streamer object should use to transfer data into the the actual fifo.
specifies a routine to use to read data from the actual FIFO. It is assumed that this read routine will handle only reads from an aligned buffer. (Otherwise, why are we using this streamer driver?)
specifies a routine to use to initiate a receive on the actual FIFO.
specifies a routine to use to retrieve the occupancy in the actual FIFO. The true occupancy value needs to come through this streamer driver because it holds some of the bytes.

memberdef.argsstring: (XStrm_TxFifoStreamer *InstancePtr, unsigned FifoWidth, void *FifoInstance, XStrm_XferFnType WriteFn, XStrm_SetLenFnType SetLenFn, XStrm_GetVacancyFnType GetVacancyFn)
memberdef.name: XStrm_TxInitialize

briefdescription.para: XStrm_TxInitialize initializes the XStrm_TxFifoStreamer object referenced by InstancePtr.

parameterdescription.para: references the tx streamer on which to operate.
specifies the FIFO keyhole size in bytes.
references the FIFO driver instance that this streamer object should use to transfer data into the the actual fifo.

specifies a routine to use to write data into the actual FIFO. It is assumed that this write routine will handle only writes from an aligned buffer. (Otherwise, why are we using this streamer driver?)
specifies a routine to use to initiate a transmit on the actual FIFO.
specifies a routine to use to retrieve the vacancy in the actual FIFO. The true vacancy value needs to come through this streamer driver because it holds some of the bytes.

briefdescription.para: XStrm_RxGetLen notifies the hardware that the program is ready to receive the next frame from the receive channel of the FIFO, specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: Note that the program must first call XStrm_RxGetLen before pulling data out of the receive channel of the FIFO with XStrm_Read.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

simplesect.para: XStrm_RxGetLen returns the number of bytes available in the next frame.

briefdescription.para: XStrm_Read reads Bytes bytes from the FIFO specified by InstancePtr to the block of memory, referenced by BufPtr.

detaileddescription.para: Care must be taken to ensure that the number of bytes read with one or more calls to XStrm_Read() does not exceed the number of bytes available given from the last call to XStrm_RxGetLen().

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.
specifies the memory address to place the data read.
specifies the number of bytes to read.

briefdescription.para: XStrm_TxSetLen flushes to the FIFO, specified by InstancePtr, any bytes remaining in internal buffers and begins a hardware transfer of data out of the transmit channel of the FIFO.

detaileddescription.para: Bytes specifies the number of bytes in the frame to transmit.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO Streamer on which to operate.
specifies the frame length in bytes.

briefdescription.para: XStrm_Write writes Bytes bytes of the block of memory, referenced by BufPtr, to the transmit channel of the FIFO referenced by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: Care must be taken to ensure that the number of bytes written with one or more calls to XStrm_Write() matches the number of bytes given in the next call to XStrm_TxSetLen().

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.
specifies the memory address of data to write.
specifies the number of bytes to write.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_Reset resets both the Tx and Rx channels and the local link interface the FIFO specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: XLlFifo_TxReset resets also sends a reset pulse to the downstream device (e.g. TEMAC). XLlFifo_Reset drops any bytes in the FIFO not yet retrieved. XLlFifo_Reset drops any bytes in the FIFO not yet transmitted.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_Status returns a bit mask of the interrupt status register (ISR) for the FIFO specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: XLlFifo_Status can be used to query the status of the FIFO without having to have interrupts enabled.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

simplesect.para: XLlFifo_IntStatus returns a bit mask of the status conditions. The mask will be a set of bitwise or'd values from the XLLF_INT_*_MASK preprocessor symbols.

memberdef.name: XLlFifo_IntEnable
memberdef.initializer: { \ u32 Reg = XLlFifo_ReadReg((InstancePtr)->BaseAddress, \ XLLF_IER_OFFSET); \ Reg |= ((Mask) & XLLF_INT_ALL_MASK); \ XLlFifo_WriteReg((InstancePtr)->BaseAddress, XLLF_IER_OFFSET, \ Reg); \ }

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_IntEnable enables the interrupts specified in Mask for the FIFO specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: The corresponding interrupt for each bit set to 1 in Mask, will be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.
contains a bit mask of the interrupts to enable. The mask can be formed using a set of bitwise or'd values from the XLLF_INT_*_MASK preprocessor symbols.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XLlFifo_IntEnable(XLlFifo *InstancePtr, u32 Mask)

memberdef.name: XLlFifo_IntDisable
memberdef.initializer: { \ u32 Reg = XLlFifo_ReadReg((InstancePtr)->BaseAddress, \ XLLF_IER_OFFSET); \ Reg &= ~((Mask) & XLLF_INT_ALL_MASK); \ XLlFifo_WriteReg((InstancePtr)->BaseAddress, XLLF_IER_OFFSET, \ Reg); \ }

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_IntDisable disables the interrupts specified in Mask for the FIFO specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: The corresponding interrupt for each bit set to 1 in Mask, will be disabled. In other words, XLlFifo_IntDisable uses the "set a bit to clear it" scheme.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.
contains a bit mask of the interrupts to disable. The mask can be formed using a set of bitwise or'd values from the XLLF_INT_*_MASK preprocessor symbols.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XLlFifo_IntDisable(XLlFifo *InstancePtr, u32 Mask)

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_IntPending returns a bit mask of the pending interrupts for the FIFO specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: Each bit set to 1 in the return value represents a pending interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

simplesect.para: XLlFifo_IntPending returns a bit mask of the interrupts that are pending. The mask will be a set of bitwise or'd values from the XLLF_INT_*_MASK preprocessor symbols.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_IntClear clears pending interrupts specified in Mask for the FIFO specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: The corresponding pending interrupt for each bit set to 1 in Mask, will be cleared. In other words, XLlFifo_IntClear uses the "set a bit to clear it" scheme.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.
contains a bit mask of the pending interrupts to clear. The mask can be formed using a set of bitwise or'd values from the XLLF_INT_*_MASK preprocessor symbols.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XLlFifo_IntClear(XLlFifo *InstancePtr, u32 Mask)

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_RxReset resets the receive channel of the FIFO specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: XLlFifo_RxReset drops any bytes in the FIFO not yet retrieved.
The calling software may want to test for the completion of the reset by reading the interrupt status (IS) register and testing for the Rx Reset complete (RRC) bit.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_IsRxEmpty returns true if the receive channel of the FIFO, specified by InstancePtr, is empty.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

simplesect.para: XLlFifo_IsRxEmpty returns TRUE when the receive channel of the FIFO is empty. Otherwise, XLlFifo_IsRxEmpty returns FALSE.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_RxOccupancy returns the number of 32-bit words available (occupancy) to be read from the receive channel of the FIFO, specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: The xps_ll_fifo core uses the same fifo to store data values and frame length values. Upon initialization, the XLlFifo_RxOccupancy will give the value of 1, which means one length value (a reserved fifo location) and no data values.

InstancePtr
references the FIFO on which to operate.
XLlFifo_RxOccupancy returns the occupancy count for the specified packet FIFO.
C Signature: u32 XLlFifo_RxOccupancy(XLlFifo *InstancePtr)

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_RxGetLen notifies the hardware that the program is ready to receive the next frame from the receive channel of the FIFO, specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: Note that the program must first call XLlFifo_RxGetLen before pulling data out of the receive channel of the FIFO with XLlFifo_Read.

InstancePtr
references the FIFO on which to operate.
XLlFifo_RxGetLen returns the number of bytes available in the next frame.
C Signature: u32 XLlFifo_RxGetLen(XLlFifo *InstancePtr)

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_Read reads Bytes bytes from the receive channel of the FIFO referenced by InstancePtr to the block of memory, referenced by BufPtr.

detaileddescription.para: Care must be taken to ensure that the number of bytes read with one or more calls to XLlFifo_Read() does not exceed the number of bytes available given from the last call to XLlFifo_RxGetLen().

InstancePtr
references the FIFO on which to operate.
BufPtr
specifies the memory address to place the data read.
Bytes
specifies the number of bytes to read.
N/A
Error handling is handled through hardware exceptions and interrupts. C Signature: void XLlFifo_Read(XLlFifo *InstancePtr, void *BufPtr, unsigned Bytes)

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_TxReset resets the transmit channel of the FIFO specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: XLlFifo_TxReset drops any bytes in the FIFO not yet transmitted.
The calling software may want to test for the completion of the reset by reading the interrupt status (IS) register and testing for the Tx Reset complete (TRC) bit.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_IsTxDone returns true if the transmission in the transmit channel of the FIFO, specified by InstancePtr, is complete.

detaileddescription.para: XLlFifo_IsTxDone works only if the TC bit in the IS register is cleared before sending a frame.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

simplesect.para: XLlFifo_IsTxDone returns TRUE when the transmit channel of the FIFO is complete. Otherwise, XLlFifo_IsTxDone returns FALSE.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_IsRxDone returns true if the reception in the receive channel of the FIFO, specified by InstancePtr, is complete.

detaileddescription.para: XLlFifo_IsRxDone works only if the RC bit in the ISR register is cleared before receiving a frame.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

simplesect.para: XLlFifo_IsRxDone returns TRUE when the receive channel of the FIFO is complete. Otherwise, XLlFifo_IsRxDone returns FALSE.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_TxVacancy returns the number of unused 32 bit words available (vacancy) in the send channel of the FIFO specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: The xps_ll_fifo core uses tXLLF_he same fifo to store data values and frame length values. Upon initialization, the XLlFifo_TxVacancy will give the value of FIFO_WIDTH - 1, which means one length value used (a reserved fifo location) and no data values yet present.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

simplesect.para: XLlFifo_TxVacancy returns the vacancy count in 32-bit words for the specified FIFO.

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_TxSetLen begins a hardware transfer of Bytes bytes out of the transmit channel of the FIFO specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
references the FIFO on which to operate.
Bytes
specifies the frame length in bytes.
N/A
C Signature: void XLlFifo_TxSetLen(XLlFifo *InstancePtr, u32 Bytes)

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_Write writes Bytes bytes of the block of memory, referenced by BufPtr, to the transmit channel of the FIFO referenced by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: Care must be taken to ensure that the number of bytes written with one or more calls to XLlFifo_Write() matches the number of bytes given in the next call to XLlFifo_TxSetLen().

InstancePtr
references the FIFO on which to operate.
BufPtr
specifies the memory address of data to write.
Bytes
specifies the number of bytes to write.
N/A
Error handling is handled through hardware exceptions and interrupts. C Signature: void XLlFifo_Write(XLlFifo *InstancePtr, void *BufPtr, unsigned Bytes)

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_WriteTdr writes to the Transmit Destination Register (TDR)

detaileddescription.para:
The TDR stores the destination address corresponding to the packet to be transmitted. When presenting a transmit packet to the AXI4-Stream FIFO core the following sequence should be followed
Write the destination address into TDR first,
Write the packet data to the Transmit Data FIFO next
Write the length of the packet into the Transmit Length Register.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.
is the Transmit Destination address to be written to TDR.

simplesect.para: C Signature: void XLlFifo_WriteTdr(XLlFifo *InstancePtr, u32 Tdest);

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_ReadTdr returns the contents of the Receive Destination Register(RDR).

detaileddescription.para: The RDR contains destination address corresponding to the valid packet that is received. The RDR should only be read when a receive packet is available for processing (the receive occupancy is not zero). Once the RDR is read, the receive packet data should be read from the receive data FIFO before the RDR is read again. The RDR values are stored in the receive data FIFO by the AXI4-Stream FIFO core with the data of each packet. The RDR value for the subsequent packet to be processed is moved to the RDR when the previous RDR value has been read.

parameterdescription.para: references the FIFO on which to operate.

simplesect.para: The Receive Destination address read from the RDR.

memberdef.name: XLlFifo_reg_name
memberdef.initializer: (((RegOffset) == XLLF_ISR_OFFSET) ? "ISR": \ ((RegOffset) == XLLF_IER_OFFSET) ? "IER": \ ((RegOffset) == XLLF_TDFR_OFFSET) ? "TDFR {tx reset}": \ ((RegOffset) == XLLF_TDFV_OFFSET) ? "TDFV {tx vacancy}": \ ((RegOffset) == XLLF_TDFD_OFFSET) ? "TDFD {tx data}": \ ((RegOffset) == XLLF_TLF_OFFSET) ? "TLF {tx length}": \ ((RegOffset) == XLLF_RDFR_OFFSET) ? "RDFR {rx reset}": \ ((RegOffset) == XLLF_RDFO_OFFSET) ? "RDFO {rx occupancy}": \ ((RegOffset) == XLLF_RDFD_OFFSET) ? "RDFD {rx data}": \ ((RegOffset) == XLLF_RLF_OFFSET) ? "RLF {rx length}": \ "unknown")

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_ReadReg returns the value of the register at the offset, RegOffset, from the memory mapped base address, BaseAddress.

parameterdescription.para: specifies the base address of the device.
specifies the offset from BaseAddress.

simplesect.para: XLlFifo_ReadReg returns the value of the specified register.
C-style signature: u32 XLlFifo_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: XLlFifo_WriteReg writes the value, Value, to the register at the offset, RegOffset, from the memory mapped base address, BaseAddress.

parameterdescription.para: specifies the base address of the device.
specifies the offset from BaseAddress.
is value to write to the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XLlFifo_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Value)

briefdescription.para: The Xilinx local link FIFO driver component.

detaileddescription.para: The Xilinx byte streamer for packet FIFOs.
See xtreamer.h for a description on how to use this driver.
This file contains static initialization functionality for Axi Streaming FIFO driver.
This header file contains identifiers and low-level driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the xps_ll_fifo core.
Provide a template for user to define their own hardware settings.
This driver supports the Xilinx xps_ll_fifo core.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jvb 10/13/06 First release 1.00a xd 12/17/07 Added type casting to fix CR #456850 1.02a jz 12/04/09 Hal phase 1 support 2.00a hbm 01/20/10 Hal phase 1 support, bump up major release 2.01a asa 09/17/10 Added code for resetting Streaming interface for CR574868 3.00a adk 08/10/13 Added support for AXI4 Datainterface.Updated XLlFifo_RxGetWord, XLlFifo_TxPutword inorder to handle AXI4 Datainterface. Added Config initialization for the driver. 5.1 sk 11/10/15 Used UINTPTR instead of u32 for Baseaddress CR# 867425. Changed the prototypes of XLlFifo_CfgInitialize, XLlFifo_Initialize APIs. 5.5 sk 06/15/20 In XLlFifo_iRead_Aligned and XLlFifo_iWrite_Aligned add type casting to fix gcc warnings. 5.6 sd 07/7/23 Add system devicetree support.

detaileddescription.para: If using XPS, this file will be automatically generated.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
3.00a adk 9/10/2013 initial release

detaileddescription.para: High-level driver functions are defined in xpfifo.h.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jvb 10/16/06 First release. 1.02a jz 12/04/09 Hal phase 1 support 2.00a hbm 01/20/10 Hal phase 1 support, bump up major release 2.03a asa 14/08/12 Added XLLF_TDR_OFFSET, XLLF_RDR_OFFSET defines for the new registers, and XLLF_INT_TFPF_MASK, XLLF_INT_TFPE_MASK, XLLF_INT_RFPF_MASK and XLLF_INT_RFPE_MASK for the new version of the AXI4-Stream FIFO core (v2.01a and later) 6.1 vlt 01/22/26 Fixed codespell issues

Ver Who Date Changes
3.00a adk 9/10/2013 initial release 6.1 vlt 12/12/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jvb 10/13/06 First release - based on Robert McGee's streaming packet fifo driver. 1.02a jz 12/04/09 Hal phase 1 support 2.00a hbm 01/20/10 Hal phase 1 support, bump up major release 2.02a asa 12/28/11 The function XStrm_Read is changed to reset HeadIndex to zero when all the bytes are read. 5.5 sk 06/15/20 In XStrm_Read and XStrm_Write add type casting to fix gcc warnings.

detaileddescription.para: This driver enables higher layer software to access a hardware FIFO using any alignment in the data buffers while preserving alignment for the hardware FIFO access.
This driver treats send and receive channels separately, using different types of instance objects for each.
This driver makes use of another FIFO driver to access the specific FIFO hardware through use of the routines passed into the Tx/RxInitialize routines.
Send and receive channels are initialized separately. The receive channel is initiailzed using XStrm_RxInitialize(). The send channel is initialized using XStrm_TxInitialize().

It is fairly simple to use the API provided by this byte streamer driver. The only somewhat tricky part is that the calling code must correctly call a couple routines in the right sequence for receive and transmit. This sequence is described here. Check the routine functional descriptions for information on how to use a specific API routine.

A frame is received by using the following sequence: 1) call XStrm_RxGetLen() to get the length of the next incoming frame. 2) call XStrm_Read() one or more times to read the number of bytes reported by XStrm_RxGetLen().
Other restrictions on the sequence of API calls may apply depending on the specific FIFO driver used by this byte streamer driver.

A frame is transmittted by using the following sequence: 1) call XStrm_Write() one or more times to write all the of bytes in the next frame. 2) call XStrm_TxSetLen() to begin the transmission of frame just written.
Other restrictions on the sequence of API calls may apply depending on the specific FIFO driver used by this byte streamer driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jvb 10/12/06 First release 1.02a jz 12/04/09 Hal phase 1 support 2.00a hbm 01/20/10 Hal phase 1 support, bump up major release 2.02a asa 12/28/11 The macro XStrm_IsRxInternalEmpty is changed to use FrmByteCnt instead of HeadIndex.
